content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Appointment Checklist: What To Expect Before, During and After Your Visit

  - url: https://my.clevelandclinic.org/patients/information/appointments-checklist

  - source: Cleveland Clinic

  - domain: my.clevelandclinic.org


  ## Content


  Cleveland Clinic presents this page as a general checklist for staying organized
  before, during and after an appointment.


  ## Before the appointment


  For a first visit with a new healthcare provider, patients may need to bring paperwork
  or information that is not already available in MyChart. The checklist includes:


  - copies of test results, imaging or medical records related to the current concern;

  - a completed medical history form when the provider requested one;

  - a complete list of current prescription medicines, over-the-counter medicines
  and supplements;

  - any special diet or medication instructions previously provided by the doctor.


  A patient may also consider bringing a support person and a sweater or jacket.


  ## During and after the appointment


  Patients are encouraged to discuss symptoms and concerns openly and to ask questions
  when something is unclear. Interpreter assistance may be requested. After the visit,
  the patient receives a visit summary and instructions for tests, follow-up or other
  next steps, which may also be available in MyChart.


  The page also describes appointment reminders and general check-in options. It is
  a broad appointment-preparation checklist. It does not determine which payment or
  coverage route applies to a particular patient before the visit.

  '
metadata: {}
title: 'Appointment Checklist: What To Expect Before, During and After Your Visit'
url: https://my.clevelandclinic.org/patients/information/appointments-checklist

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Costs and Payment: What To Expect Before Your Visit

  - url: https://my.clevelandclinic.org/patients/billing-finance/billing-checklist

  - source: Cleveland Clinic

  - domain: my.clevelandclinic.org


  ## Content


  Cleveland Clinic provides this checklist to help patients prepare for costs and
  payment before a visit.


  ## Insurance coverage


  Patients who intend to use insurance should confirm that Cleveland Clinic accepts
  their insurance and should check with the insurance company about what is covered.
  They should also determine whether pre-authorization is required. A referral may
  be required by the insurance company even though Cleveland Clinic itself does not
  require one for every visit.


  Patients should review possible copays, deductibles, coinsurance and out-of-pocket
  maximums before arriving. They may also request a pre-service cost estimate. An
  estimate is not a final bill because the final cost can depend on the care provided.


  ## When insurance is not being used


  The page separately addresses patients who do not have health insurance or are not
  using insurance for the visit. Those patients may request a Good Faith Estimate
  for scheduled services and may review self-pay or financial-assistance options.


  The applicable financial preparation therefore depends on whether the patient plans
  to use insurance for the visit or will follow a self-pay route. The page does not
  identify which route a particular patient intends to use.

  '
metadata: {}

content.content: I need user clarification or authorization before continuing, so I will stop here until the user responds.

Your first in-person new-patient visit is scheduled for **August 4, 2026 at 10:00 a.m.** at **Cleveland Clinic Main Campus**. Since you plan to use insurance, here’s a practical checklist:

### Before the visit
- **Confirm your insurance is accepted** by Cleveland Clinic and check whether your plan requires prior authorization or a referral.
- Review your expected **copay, deductible, and coinsurance**.
- Have your **insurance card** and photo ID ready.
- Complete any new-patient forms available through the clinic or patient portal.
- Gather:
  - A list of all prescription medicines, over-the-counter medicines and supplements, including doses if possible.
  - Relevant medical records, test results, imaging reports or images.
  - Your medical and surgical history, allergies and vaccination information.
  - A short list of symptoms, concerns and questions.
- Make a note of any special diet or medication instructions you’ve already received. Don’t change medications just for the appointment unless a clinician instructs you to.
- Plan transportation and allow extra time for parking and check-in. Bring a support person or request interpreter assistance if needed.

### At the visit
Be ready to describe when your concerns began, what makes them better or worse, and how they affect you. Ask about anything unclear and make sure you understand the follow-up plan before leaving.

Cleveland Clinic’s general preparation guidance: [Appointment Checklist](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/patients/information/appointments-checklist) and [Billing and Payment Checklist](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/patients/billing-finance/billing-checklist).

final_answer:
Your first in-person new-patient visit is scheduled for **August 4, 2026 at 10:00 a.m.** at **Cleveland Clinic Main Campus**. Since you plan to use insurance, here’s a practical checklist:

### Before the visit
- **Confirm your insurance is accepted** by Cleveland Clinic and check whether your plan requires prior authorization or a referral.
- Review your expected **copay, deductible, and coinsurance**.
- Have your **insurance card** and photo ID ready.
- Complete any new-patient forms available through the clinic or patient portal.
- Gather:
  - A list of all prescription medicines, over-the-counter medicines and supplements, including doses if possible.
  - Relevant medical records, test results, imaging reports or images.
  - Your medical and surgical history, allergies and vaccination information.
  - A short list of symptoms, concerns and questions.
- Make a note of any special diet or medication instructions you’ve already received. Don’t change medications just for the appointment unless a clinician instructs you to.
- Plan transportation and allow extra time for parking and check-in. Bring a support person or request interpreter assistance if needed.

### At the visit
Be ready to describe when your concerns began, what makes them better or worse, and how they affect you. Ask about anything unclear and make sure you understand the follow-up plan before leaving.

Cleveland Clinic’s general preparation guidance: [Appointment Checklist](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/patients/information/appointments-checklist) and [Billing and Payment Checklist](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/patients/billing-finance/billing-checklist).
